summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the default GFM emphasis parser path repeatedly rescans long unclosed `*` runs and exhibits a dramatic parser-side CPU slowdown on the pinned vulnerable tag.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that a 56,001-byte unclosed-emphasis payload takes at least 500 ms to parse and remains at least 100x slower than same-size plain text, demonstrating a repository-owned availability impact rather than ordinary large-input cost.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned `markedjs/marked` at `v18.0.1`, built the parser in the container, and measured the attack payload at a 2,022.062 ms median parse time versus a 0.505 ms median for same-size plain text, a 4,001.222x slowdown. The verifier reported success.
verification_method: The helper copies the pinned repository into a writable workspace, installs dependencies from the bundled lockfile, builds the ESM output, then times three parses of a same-size plain-text control and three parses of the unclosed-emphasis attack payload in the same Node process. Verification requires an attack median of at least 500 ms and a slowdown ratio of at least 100x.
evidence_produced_by_run: Verifier output recorded `control_times_ms` of `[0.664, 0.505, 0.465]` and `attack_times_ms` of `[2022.062, 1984.851, 2108.636]`.
The median control parse time was `0.505 ms`, the median attack parse time was `2022.062 ms`, and the measured slowdown ratio was `4001.222`.
The final `./reproduction/run.sh` invocation ended with `[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
